macro_segments.title: Bathing restrictions on Tisha B'Av versus mourning (Avelut): hot water versus cold water

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the debate: תענית versus אבילות restrictions on bathing
Permitted exceptions for תענית: daytime work prohibition and wearing shoes outside the city
Bathing restrictions comparison: hands, face, feet allowed in both תענית and אבילות
Parallel of מנודה and אבל to תענית; connection to משנה themes of rejection
Proof from ברייתא equating אבל and תענית bathing rules; cold water must be forbidden
Rav Sheshet's stringency: אבל cannot even dip a finger in hot water
Gemara's attempt to distinguish: תענית only forbids hot water, אבל forbids cold water

macro_segments.title: Supporting evidence and challenges to the cold water prohibition for אבל

micro_segments.title: Story of Rabi Yosi ben Rabi Chanina's son bathing in cold water during mourning
Gemara's response: exception due to multiple consecutive mourning periods
Allowed hair and clothing care under restrictions (razor not scissors, water without soap)
Rava's position: אבל is permitted to bathe in cold water all seven days
Rava's argument: meat and wine are permitted, so אבילות is not about physical suffering

macro_segments.title: Challenging Rava's position with the bogeret (mature unmarried woman) distinction

micro_segments.title: Bogeret cannot dishevel herself during mourning her father; marriage prospects concern
Gemara's analysis: if hot water is forbidden, bogeret cannot bathe in hot water
Gemara's conclusion: must be discussing cold water as the permitted exception
Alternative explanation: discussion is about makeup, not water bathing

macro_segments.title: Debating צער (suffering) as the basis for אבילות restrictions, and exemptions for meat and wine

micro_segments.title: Logical parallel: if cold water forbidden due to צער, then meat and wine should be forbidden
Gemara's resolution: meat and wine are exceptions to deal with anxiety and overwhelming emotions
Fundamental assumption accepted: אבילות restrictions are based on צער (suffering)

macro_segments.title: Final halachic conclusions on bathing and anointing for אבל and on Tisha B'Av

micro_segments.title: Conclusion: אבל forbidden even cold water, with allowance for hands, face, feet
Anointing (סיכה) forbidden even minimally for אבל, exception only to remove stench
Parallel allowances for Tisha B'Av: hot water restrictions with exceptions for cleanliness

macro_segments.title: Where to recite עננו (Aneinu) in the Shemoneh Esrei for individual and communal fasts

micro_segments.title: Individual accepting תענית must recite special עננו prayer; location between Goel and Rofei
Rav Yitzchak's challenge: individual cannot establish a new blessing independently
Resolution: individual inserts עננו in שומע תפילה; שליח ציבור adds separate blessing

macro_segments.title: Reconciling contradictory beraitot about whether pregnant and nursing women fast

micro_segments.title: First braita: pregnant and nursing women fast in early fasts, not late ones
Second braita: pregnant and nursing women fast in late fasts, not early ones
Third braita: pregnant and nursing women do not fast in early or late fasts
Rav Ashi's solution: take the middle fasts as reference point; all three braitot reconciled

topical_tags.term: meat and wine (בשר ויין - basar veyayin)
שליח ציבור (shliach tzibur) - communal prayer leader
